channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Bitcoin or altcoins? — 86 replies — 135.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 86 • Updated: 2026-05-13 06:49 UTC

OP

If you had to pick, what would you buy right now: BTC or altcoins? I’m not seeing much ROI in alts lately, even utility or memecoins.

Early replies (first 3)

62195293
>>62195275
Bitcoin until 120k

62195300
you can't time the market I thought like >>62195293
last time, bitcoin to 70k and sell half and go into alts and then 2021 alts just fucking died for a year.

The alts that pumped are things that haven't gone parabolic yet, or big projects like Solana with glowie money attached to them. Ones like Link are not going to 1000 regardless of cope.

/biz/ will pretty much only give you bad info too. I've found maybe 3 coins here that were Guaranteed returns, everything else I had to research myself. In fact I remember last year posting proof that CRCL stock was going to 10x on IPO and got XRP schizos fudding me out of taking a life changing position.

I actually hate the fuck out of baggies.

62195373
>>62195293
Why 120k only?? Why not 1M?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62209253
>>62208030
Yeah and still think the space matures eventually once better infrastructure and security become the priority.

62209260
>>62208168
Are you also holding?

62209263
>>62207947
Yeah true, but can’t really generalize everything either. Some protocols got hacked for completely different reasons too.

62209287
>>62208072
days over? You're lying

62209291
>>62209260
Hodl

62209304
>>62195275
BTC dominance still looks too strong for a full alt run right now.

62209376
>>62207965
Let's see how it goes

62209379
>>62204588
So are you into btc?

62209385
Bitcoin is getting fucked

62209525
>>62209385
Utility coins barely move unless the market gets euphoric again. Just btc

62209590
People pretending fundamentals matter in crypto will never stop being funny.

62209854
>>62195275
Everyone becomes a macro analyst after holding one red candle lmao

62210047
>>62209304
but when?

62210050
>>62209291
hold and stake

62211194
>>62207855
I've got some hbar in my portfolio

62211429
>>62211194
hbar going down to 2c before it ever gets a chance at real traction

62211440
>>62195275
>Chainlink is the future

62211870
>>62211440
Hahaha

62211903
>>62195275
Everyone moved to BTC of course, I dumped my ETH for BTC too, I used to hold BTC because of staking but now I can stake BTC natively thanks to TBV and the likes

62211915
>>62195275
I’d rather keep stacking BTC, then put it to work via native staking / yield layers (like Babylon-style setups) instead of chasing dead charts and hoping for a narrative pump.

62211928
>>62209385
Ok as you say so

62211932
>>62211429
Hbar is okay to hold and buy

62212034
>>62195275
More potential upside on alts for the next six months. Specifically memes right now.

62213245
>>62211440
What's your price target anon?

62214696
>>62211932
All in or?

62216973
>>62211915
It's worth considering anon

62216987
>>62195275

Polkadot

62220595
>>62209854
Are you sure?

62224657
>>62216987
Why polkadot?

62224666
cny/usd is > .147 now w/trump headed to china. every penny that can chase risk on is gonna get horned up immediately. The ONLY thing the US has left is the dollar as a w3@p0n. Plus, i can't afford any more makers mark bottles unless my alts start pumping w/haste, so...

item.title: First Propert Financial Advice — 24 replies — 96.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
First Propert Financial Advice

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-05-13 07:13 UTC

OP

>32 years old
>boomer dad is willing to buy me a townhome in June of 2027 for 400k or less.
>I have a TERRIBLE credit score and own zero credit cards, hence why I’m 32 and need help buying my first property.
>boomer dad is very greedy and selfish and will probably put the property under his name with perfect credit score and I am afraid that I will be fucked when he passes if I have to do a bunch of paperwork or lawyers or pay money to get it in my name with my shit score

Any advice on if I should try to live there forever so I never go homeless? Or should I sell it after some years and invest the money? Any other financial advise would be greatly appreciated.

Also he refuses to get me any sort of real house which is my true happiness dream to live in the woods in a house even if small/medium. He said it had to be a condo/apartment for 400k or less and I convinced him to at least a townhome. So I could sell it eventually for a dream house too but I would have a tough time keeping up with an HOA or basic maintenance

Early replies (first 3)

62214442
>>62214415
Is there any reason why your father would give it away to someone else (or sell it and give the money to someone else) when he dies?

My mother has been financially abusive to me (and mentally and physically too) for my whole life. I'm the one stuck taking care of her in her dying years but she keeps threatening to disinherit me every time I want to go visit a friend or don't rush off to the store instantly to go buy her donuts when she runs out.

Anyway, you always need a place to live, and home ownership is your hedge against inflation. Doesn't work so well with condos because of the HOA fees. Doesn't really work all that well with anything because of property taxes, which cities/counties/states don't give a fuck about raising on you until you're forced out. My mother had to sell my childhood home because taxes went up to $30,000 per year on it (the suburb decided it had to build a waterpark to compete with the suburb next door that had built a giant waterpark, and the money to build that thing had to come from somewhere).

62214447
>>62214415
>boomer dad is willing to buy me a townhome in June of 2027 for 400k or less.
>boomer dad is very greedy and selfish

So which is it? You sound like a massive faggot and I would've kicked you out for being gay.

62214449
>>62214415
>he refuses to get me any sort of real house which is my true happiness dream
Your dad is based. You NEED to achieve your own goal on your own terms. He is giving you a massive boost to do so. Be grateful no matter the path he picks but feel free to voice your concerns but make sure to mention you respect his decision and are thankful. You can achieve your dream anon, just be willing to do something for it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62214562
>>62214415
you are 32... you have been out of school for 14 years, and you have experienced a bull market just about every year of your adult life

on top of that, you have a dad who is willing to give you free rent... yet you're bitching about it being in his name

he is correct to demand an condo/townhouse - he recognizes you as a worthless irresponsible fuck who cannot keep up with the maintenance of a house

62214574
>my dad will buy me a house to live in
>my dad is very greedy
you sound like a little bitch

62214581
>>62214562
>you have experienced a bull market just about every year of your adult life

Not true, i bought eth in 2023-2024 and i’m not up on my investment in any way.

62214652
>>62214415
>0 cards
>TERRIBLE score
So what did you do here, take out a CC when you were 19 and then spend 120k and default or something.

62214659
>>62214562
>you are 32... you have been out of school for 14 years, and you have experienced a bull market just about every year of your adult life
Stock bull markets don't do anything for kids who have no money to buy low.
>out of school
Highschool degrees are functionally worthless except for working at walmart.
If he graduated 10 years ago he would have gotten anally raped by the bad covid job market and then struggle after the tech bubble burst unless he's one of those larpers who claims he makes 300k from home.
>free rent
His dad should pay him reparations for being born at all.

62214877
>>62214442
I understand the abusive parent. Thats what happened with me. Its a good point "we will always need somewhere to live" but im terrified of taxes and hoa like you said.

>>62214447
Looking back on my OP post, absolute faggotry the way I wrote it. I realized that. but also you don't know the full story. Just to start, the guy abused me as child then kicked me out when I was 20 and gave me $150 to live off and basically be homeless. Changed all house locks on me. Set me up for failure right away in life. i did nothing to him. My original post is written terribly.

>>62214449
thank you

>>62214562
i am bitching because hes going to die at anytime, he has ongoing cancer and is almost 70 and has millions of dollars but refuses to set me up while he's still alive. His only son. So I think its very selfish to want to put the condo/townhome in his name right before he dies as if he's going to just flip it one day for himself instead of giving my a gift finally. Just incredibly selfish in my opinion that he wouldn't secure it in his sons before he dies so I have a home when he passes, and just have to worry about taxes and such.

>>62214574
both those statements stand, and reading back my OP post, i wrote it like a bitch, but yeah both are true. He has millions of dollars, and wouldn't consider getting me a condo or townhome or even money to survive off once. I just think if I was a millionare with a son, i would get set him up for life.

62219930
>>62214877
>Just to start, the guy abused me as child then kicked me out when I was 20 and gave me $150 to live off and basically be homeless.
You mean you didn't go to college at 18?
You mean you didn't start working at 16?
Sounds very tolerant of him.
Sounds like you just stayed at home and smoked weed all day.

62219988
>>62214415
you again nigger, kys

62220282
>>62214877

Reading all of this, it sounds like you didn't do anything useful with your time since you were 16 till now.

> I just think if I was a millionare with a son, i would get set him up for life.

That is your belief when you yourself have fuck all to give.

62220292
>>62214877
>start post apologizing
>end post vehemently insisting you did nothing wrong

Hey OP? I hope you end up getting raped to death by wild coyotes you pink poodle bitch.

62220431
>>62214415
>I have a TERRIBLE credit score and own zero credit cards
you sound based as fuck, my friend. Fuck this jewsih world and its jewish games and lies

62223176
You should live with your dad even if he could buy you a townhouse, you should be living with him, why do you need your own house? Lol. If I had a son I'd just tell him to live with me and not pay rent, there's no point in having a seperate house.

Family is good to live with anyways, gives you someone to talk to and socialize with.

Also, he should at the very least, if he's rich, set aside 350k in an ETF for you to grow and he should regularly show you how much is in it but he needs to make sure that you're responsible enough to not spend that money and let it grow.

Thats what I would do if I had a son.

62223188
>>62214877
Your dad does sound a little selfish. If I have a kid I am leaving them everything and instructing them on how to use the money.

Does he have a will and are you the primary beneficiary?

62223199
>>62214659
>whiny turbo negative bitch
>anti natalist
Like clockwork

62223407
>>62214442
News flash: you're not getting any inheritance.
Move out, move to a different state, tell your mother to take out a reverse mortgage on her house and use the money for a philippina caretaker.

62224581
>>62223176
>>62223188
If you had a son and he kept smoking your medical marijuana instead of buying his own, you'd kick his ass out, too.

62224590
>>62214415
boomer here

the way I see it you're going to lose everything I give you because you didn't earn it yourself so you don't know how to keep it.

since you're going to lose everything I give you, I might as well not give you anything

go out and get your own stuff then you won't lose it when I die. Best of luck. If I can help let me know but i'm not going to carry you over the finish line because that harms you more than it helps.

62224620
>>62223199
>boomer internet defense force
>"JUST IGNORE THE 10 DOLLAR A GALLON GAS, GOY, BOOMERS DINDU NUFFIN"
If you said the dumb shit you say here in real life you'd be beaten within an inch of your life you terminal contrarian dipshit.

62224624
>>62214877
Your dad is a retard and the responses in this thread and others like "just buy voo" just shows most of the userbase has been replaced with seething redditors and terminal contrarians who suck boomer dick all day just because everyone else knows how cancerous they are.

62224640
>>62224620
>>62224624
You aren't living in the real world and people giving real world advice aren't contrarians.

62224713
>>62224620

item.title: Australia is fucked — 22 replies — 88.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Australia is fucked

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-05-13 07:15 UTC

OP

>CGT discount - DENIED
>Negative Gearing - DENIED
Where were you when the Australian dream died?

Early replies (first 3)

62224090
have the liberals said they will reverse it? do we just hold and wait?

62224122
>>62223850
Doesn’t this come into effect in 2027? Should I sell all my crypto before 2027?

62224148
>>62223850
its tiring living here
the new rules favour low return long duration assets. high growth assets like btc and leveraged etfs get smoked compared old rules. run a few tests... under 12 months nothing changes.
i wonder if it actually encourages speculation. might as well punt on options or asx bets miners or U.S letfs... you need BIG wins now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224204
>>62223850
Negative gearing has always been retarded.

62224281
>>62223850
Explain whats changed so i can care or not ?
t aussie

62224288
>>62223850
>the Australian dream
KEK
Australia was a prison land where we sent the scum to so they can starve or kill each other.
Only after the jews mettled did they get rights.

62224331
>>62224281
No more long term CGT discount. Minimum taxed is now always 30% even if you used to be in lower bracket

62224341
>>62224288
>where we sent
I really hope this isn't a bong posting, because lol if you think bongs are in any position to be talking like this in current year.

62224346
>>62224288
Shalom!

62224365
Relax, the changes only apply to new purchases, as long as you already have your properties you'll be paying less tax than future property owners.

62224398
>>62224346
>doesn't even reach half a billion
LOL

62224409
CGT changes for all asset types is retarded and makes it so property is still the best investment
My cope is that negative gearing changes will result in cheaper properties but higher yields as rental supply drops

62224440
>>62224365
dont care about properties, i want to know if shares are grandfathered in

62224467
>>62223850
SOMEONE EXPLAIN THIS TO ME IN ENGLISH
JUST HOW COOKED ARE WE CUNTS???

62224496
>>62224331
Right thanks m8.

62224499
>>62224440
This, except crypto and boomer rocks.

62224536
>>62224467
Return to pre-1999 CGT system.

62224566
>>62224536
CGT is theft, Fuck you albo.

62224682
>>62223850
I voted for this.

62224702
>>62224536
but... w why they do this do this to us

62224712
I'm of two minds on one hand I have about 600k AUD of cgt obligations which if I were to use the discount while I can would have to lock in maybe 100k of tax this coming year (yes I'll be at 45% bracket because I'm not a brokie)

On the other hand I'm priced out of the property market (not really but I don't want to pay 900k for a shitty Sydney apartment) that any fucking over of property investors is actually a good thing (for me and people my age)

62224719
>>62224712
Doesnt making $600k in profit automatically put you in the highest tax bracket owed regardless of how much you earned from waging?

item.title: Solana memes are back - 5k low cap gem edition — 17 replies — 66.6/hr

Solana memes are back - 5k low cap gem edition

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-13 07:12 UTC

OP

BOBO looks like it will reach 20m soon

Surely the og BOBINA has a chance to reach 1m

rite guise?

Early replies (first 3)

62224478
>>62224447
i remember this one

62224515
What about ETH

62224530
>>62224447
Of all the icons they choose this ? Not even a red shirt ? or a bob hair style ?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224534
>>62224530
its not the real one

62224539
>>62224530
>>62224534
It is the first one which means the other ones are a copy

62224543
>>62224539
I guess that kind of thinking makes sense if you're a 3rd worlder

62224548
>>62224543
timestamps dont lie anon, it was deployed first on SOL

62224558
When was the last time biz sent anything above 10k

Apu?

62224560
>>62224548
Meaningless if you cant meme properly.

62224565
>>62224548
It's not even congruent to the Bobina meme

62224568
>>62224560
This is true

62224573
>>62224565
I think it inspired that rendition of bobina

62224579
>>62224447
Basterrd saaar, dev nut scammer saar!

62224595
>>62224447
1m? Raise your targets.

Bobillions.

62224680
kek that shit was like a movie

62224706
>>62224447
>Saaar, sol memes are back because I pooped with my shitcoin!

62224710
you Indians are pathetic scammers

immediate izzat loss here, we can smell you through the screen

item.title: Labour shortage — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Labour shortage

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-13 07:14 UTC

OP

How is there a labour shortage if you apply on indeed and there's 2000 people applying for the same job? For every 300 jobs you apply you get 1 interview and they act like you've giving an interview for the position of the CEO of a transnational mega corporation. Why are they so fucking picky if there's a labour shortage? I remember giving a interview to be a bus boy and I told the interviewer that I thought I'd find the job easy and he got angry and said it was very difficult job. I never got called back. I worked a dead end job for 2 years and somehow I got it while applying for another job initially. You had to be able to work between anytime from 5am to 1am and work 60 hours a week and almost everyone working there was an asylum seeker living in hotels who had come on student visas. They were all planning on either saving up enough money and then going back to their countries or planned on entering the US illegally and getting bank loans (free money) from american banks. I quit the job since it was going no where and I needed a better paying job. Now I'm looking for a job for the summer before I start my last year of college but I am reminded of how fucked the job market is.

Early replies (first 3)

62224241
>>62224230
I have 70k CAD. How can I third world max?

62224247
>>62224230
who told you there was a labor shortage

62224252
>>62224247
The government telling me the reason for why we have mass migration. Apparently it's due to a labour shortage.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224271
>>62224252
there's a labor shortage of farm workers, meat processors, etc. jobs that you won't do.

62224278
>>62224230
There is no shortage, the corporations just want to pay less for labor so they get the government to flood the market with turd worlders.
>>62224271
I'd pack meat for the right price, problem is they don't wanna pay that price. If I offered you the job of sucking my dick for $5 a day, and you refused, suddenly there's a labor shortage.

62224391
>>62224278
>the right price
you mean the market price?

62224410
There's a skilled labor shortage and it makes things annoying in the blue collar world. But it's also very lucrative if you have the skills and an ounce of financial discipline.

62224415
>>62224230
This image depicts hundreds of asylum seekers lining up outside the Jacob K. Javits Federal Building in New York City.NYC Migrant Crisis: The scene illustrates the overwhelming influx of migrants straining New York City's infrastructure and shelter system.Housing Challenges: In response to the crisis, city officials have utilized various locations for housing, including tent cities on Randalls Island, hotels, and a former police academy.Policy Changes: NYC has abandoned its blanket "right to shelter" policy, implementing 30- and 60-day notices for adult migrants to find alternative housing.

62224442
>>62224410
>skilled labor shortage
In what fields?

62224717
This is why we need Indian CEOs.

item.title: I have zero respect for US poors. They do it to themselves at the end of the day. Imagine coming home from your minimum… — 18 replies — 30.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
I have zero respect for US poors. They do it to themselves at the end of the day. Imagine coming home from your minimum…

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-13 06:51 UTC

OP

I have zero respect for US poors. They do it to themselves at the end of the day. Imagine coming home from your minimum wage gas station job just to spend $30 + tip on shitty cold food from Doordash. Rice and beans, people.

Early replies (first 3)

62224352
>>62224350
Actual redneck here: we couldn't afford takeout food. We had a garden and a chicken pen and I sold eggs by the roadside and donated the money for groceries so we could afford something to eat once in awhile besides god damn fucking chicken. But I was never hungry, and we never went into debt.

62224356
>>62224350
That's a child
Probably a boy
What the fuck is wrong with you, faggot

62224363
>>62224350
>>62224352
>>62224356
rolling for a different color ID

let's see if it's green

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224366
>>62224363
Neat tbdesu

62224432
>>62224350
If you know you're never going to have enough you might as well enjoy what you got.

saving won't make you rick in the US. For most people it's not even an option. They don't earn enough to save and there's no way to cut their costs enough to save.

this will probably happen to you too, but for now your parents protect you from the realities of life. Poor people at least are making it without mommy and daddy supporting them. Might be on welfare, but that's not enough to survive. They're fighting to survive and still having their little luxuries while you sit in a safe harbor blissfully unaware that when your boomer parents reverse mortgage your house and die you'll probably be sucking dick in the park for meth and some cold leftover chicken wings.

62224450
>>62224432
>when your boomer parents reverse mortgage your house and die
This sounds like a load of projection anon. When you can't even afford a $1000 surprise expense, that's a problem and it's one that can be solved easily by not wasting your money on frivolous shit. But feel free to not take any responsibility for your position, see how far that gets you in life.

62224468
>>62224450
I'm your boomer parent, I'm doing just fine.

but I have a kid like most of you guys, and he's going to be fucked when he has to survive without me because the US is no longer affordable for single young men with no particular skills and not much drive.

62224492
>>62224468
Your kid you failed to raise is going to fuck up. Stop projecting that onto anons who preach austerity. Often times we preach it because we've had to pull ourselves up with it.

62224498
>>62224492
>Often times we preach it because we've had to pull ourselves up with it.
like I tell my kid, saving won't make you rich. Living like a poor person won't make you rich.

you guys fundamentally misunderstand what it takes to be rich. Like OP says.

it doesn't matter if you save it doesn't matter if you spend it doesn't matter if you cut coupons and recycle beer cans. None of that will ever make you rich. It probably won't even pay the bills.

62224501
>>62224468
lol stop projecting and raise your kid better wtf
i have 0 compassion for shitty parents like you and my own who literally ruined da west

62224507
>>62224498
I went from $20 net wealth to half a million net wealth despite two major medical expenses and covid being a part of that. I own my own land and have plenty of food storage and prep in case of any short term issues as well. This took me about 8 years and I was never a very high income earner. Saving and investing is how I've gotten to this point.

You teach your kid retarded lessons, no wonder he'll fail.

62224509
>>62224501
I'm not looking for your compassion.

I am telling you that you cannot make it doing what we did. It no longer works. And when you are forced to realize that, you'll gain a lot more sympathy for poor people because you will very much be one of them

62224513
>>62224507
I'm not going to shit on what you've done because you're proud of it and it's better than most.

but if you think that's enough you're in for some more very rough lessons. You are still poor.

62224600
>>62224513
>You are still poor
He's rich enough for coastfire, even leanfire as well.

62224604
>>62224600
right up until he has a medical emergency, a prolonged loss of income, or just gets sued

even if you manage to escape debt, everything about our society is designed to force you back into debt for things you never agreed to.

inb4 insurance
that has caps
on purpose
and those caps are low

62224607
>>62224350

My T levels would drop to zero on rice and beans and I’d feel depressed and estrogenic all the time. I’m not asian so I can’t handle it. I need red meat

62224608
>>62224600
or to be short,

he's one family member cancer diagnosis away from losing everything. We all are.

62224673
>>62224432
>If you know you're never going to have enough you might as well enjoy what you got.
Typical poorfag defeatist mentality, this is the real reason some people stay poor forever no matter what opportunities might come their way.

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 15 replies — 27.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
You should be holding at least 1 BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-05-13 06:54 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62220146
I have 5 BTC in cold storage. Am I gonna make it??

62220158
>>62220146
definitely. but also because of your eth stack, with just 5 BTC no.

62221733
>>62219942
Its ok. I have monkey NFTs. They are the future

62222391
>>62219945
noisee!!! Show me your proof anon

62222489
>>62219942
I hold
0.24 BTC
6k XRP
12 LTC

AIGMI?

62222594
I'm afraid of quantum computing

62223021
>>62219942
I have 1 Bitcoin and it doesn't feel like enough. It takes so much for it to go over 100k

62223087
>>62222489
no, that portfolio is far too retarded. kys

62223238
>>62219995
You can aim more if you want

62224650
>>62220027
You should also keep accumulating anon

62224655
dump it to $30k and the risk reward looks decent..

62224679
The backlash of gen z faggots realizing they slandered their best years not actually living and frugally tormenting themselves buying imaginary totally wortless fugazi will be glorious.

item.title: The BIGGEST Crypto Integration in History — 10 replies — 20.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
The BIGGEST Crypto Integration in History

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-13 06:58 UTC

OP

Historic moment as Chainlink services have been selected to be used by the DTCC! This is monumental to the crypto and blockchain industry. They even pinned the tweet! Wow, to think how far we have come as an industry.

Early replies (first 3)

62224616
Is this good or bad?

62224627
when are people going to wake up and realize that chainlink is the only crypto that matters? when will the fucking price finally go up?

62224642
>>62224627
I think you honestly really needed the spoon feeding from autists here back in 2017 to gather the confidence we have in Link. By the time Link got on the crypto map in 2020 or whenever people were cemented in their cliques for whatever coin they believed in. Most of the crypto space are normalfaggots. I’ve gotten a handful of people to buy, but you can never tell them to go all in cuz they are not emotionally invested in link. I got6 people to invest 300, 300, 700, and 500 and 400 and 1700. Almost all of them

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224644
>>62224642
Could invest more and I’ve told them too. And they basically know I’m right but they don’t care.

62224651
>word salad by company I never heard of

not impressed

62224656
>>62224577
Great news
How many tokens does the CRE require for the DTCC operations?

62224660
>>62224651
DTCC is kind of a big deal fuddy. By the way chainlink is up slightly while btc and others are down.

62224661

62224687
Up go price make this will

62224688
>>62224661

62217118

62217182

Anything. I'm currently buying work supplies for my business with XMR. That compounded with how much I've made my business is basically free and I'm making a couple thousand dollars a week.

62222692
>>62214009

62222705
>>62217182

fuck central banks
fuck fiat

62222769

62222781

if God is with us, who can be against us

62222813

62223307

62223522

62223640

item.title: Cerebras IPO May 14th edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: so you feel like in the mo… — 309 replies — 70.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Cerebras IPO May 14th edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: so you feel like in the mo…

Open thread • Replies: 309 • Updated: 2026-05-13 03:02 UTC

OP

Cerebras IPO May 14th edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners: so you feel like in the movies
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101: so zoomers with meme stocks can outperform you
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

>NEW THREAD: Copy-paste the following when making a new thread:
https://pastebin.com/NyzbAsX4

PREVIOUS
https://boards.4chan.org/biz/thread/62217769/smg-stock-market-general#top

Early replies (first 3)

62218691
> futures

62218697
AVGO, SITM, DVN & GIS
WY to 30 eventually

62218700
INTC ain’t stopping until 2T in market cap. Believe it (dattebayo)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62219835
>>62219564
I'm charging it out at as a variation with a floating average for larger clients.
And an inflated fixed cost average for smaller clients.

It took a lot of negotiations initially but the reality set in to developers.
My diesel costs per month went from $30k to nearly $80k. I have staff vehicles as well as machines

62219848
>>62219835
>>62219570
for

62219849
>>62218685
Anyone buying this IPO?

I don't want to be a goyim.

62219854
I have an important question

With ibkr

can you buy fractional shares with Japanese stocks? By fractional, I mean, can you buy 1 share? It's hard for me to hit my exact balance with Japanese stocks when I have to buy 100 lot shares. I know ibkr used to require you to buy 100x lots of Japanese shares. I just wonder if they let you buy 1 shares, instead of forcing 100. Its hard to weight this shit when 100 shares of some JP companies is $50,000 lol

62219860
>>62219854
No.
The asian Stock Exchanges have minimums

62219876
>>62219860
Are you sure? Because I checked online, and some sources said yes, and others no

Did you try to buy 1 JP shares on IBKR in the last year or less?

62219878
>>62219860
And by the way, it doesnt matter if the asian stock exchanges require minimums if the broker does fractional shares. I want to know if IBKR specifically does fractional shares for JP stocks.

62219882
>>62219854
If you're a Euro, use Trading 212

62219884
>>62219876
>Did you try to buy 1 JP shares on IBKR in the last year or less?
How about right now?

62219889
now the market wants to dip

62219892
>>62219882
>>62219860
>>62219884
But Gemini says yes

62219894
>>62219889

62219897
>>62219892
>But Gemini says yes
Why are you asking AI?
And why are you asking us?
You can either do it or you can't, and you can't.

62219906
>>62219884
Check your settings in trading permissions. See if you have fractional shares enabled.

62219909
>>62219906
No, you.

62219914
>>62219909
Please

62219922
>>62219909
If you do it, Ill tell you what JP tickers I want to buy

62219931
>>62219922
You need an account registered as "expert trader" to unlock the option.
So if you want to try it out, you'll have to lie and claim you're an expert with over 10 years experience

62219938
>>62219931
>He isn't an expert with over 10 years experience

NGMI

62219986
>>62219931
why wont you just lie?

62220010
>>62219986
Because an investor only has so many lies in a day and he needs to save them for explaining to the wife whete all their money went.

62220032
>>62220010
thats bullshit
but I believe it

62220034
>>62219986
Because I told the truth about my experience when I opened the account. Now I have to make up some lie to my broker how I instantly gained 8 years experience to show an anonymous frog whether he can buy fractional anime shares.

62220186
why are prediction markets legal? It's literally gambling, and zero sum

62224103
why didn't anons kill the thread?

item.title: NEW TRUMP POST — 21 replies — 20.8/hr

62224296

62224311

62224318
>>62224311

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224322
>>62224311
digits and market closes +2.4% tomorrow

62224324
>>62224296
You secure that shit anon.

62224334
>>62224296
all the for the market to contract 0.55%

62224335
>>62224311

62224338
>>62224322

62224348
The day the market died is upon us

62224384
>>62224292
This means all of the most powerful men in the west are going to beg China and Xi to help them. A humiliation ritual of epic proportions just like the Iran "War".

62224414
>>62224292
This feels extremely bearish.. Trump and his boys are kissing Chinese ass?

62224422
>>62224414
market is not going to read it that way though

62224427
Sell the news

62224428
>>62224296
Doesnt matter, its already priced in

62224431
why would he ask jensen to leave mid flight?

62224457
>>62224422

62224582
>>62224431

62224592
>>62224292
New fagshit dropped oooooooooooooooooo

62224594
>>62224592
Sry forgot pic

62224597
>>62224292
>''american'' billionaires fly to China to beg in person for them to loose up their asses even by little
everything they possibly offer technologically and resource wise is already in China, unless they offer up (you) and chinamen will go for it for some reason. And Taiwan too but that's not a "deal" that's hanging up white flag and letting go of US influence in east asia

62224617
>>62224292
that's why I don't envy these CEOs that much. imagine having to share a private plane with smelly donald trump just to get humiliated in china by people who prepared 1000 times better for this meeting than the trump administration is capable of. all that while they certainly have "the cards" and while you have to agree with a dementia patient that the USA is outperforming everyone in everything. literally nobody wants to be on that trip.

item.title: Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio… — 21 replies — 13.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio…

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-05-13 05:55 UTC

OP

Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main question is at the bottom below the dashed line, so you can skip all of this text if you want.

Is anyone here good with statistics or math? Idk the correct field that would apply here, some math shit.
I’ve been working on a strategy for years at this point, but I’ve been focused on a single thing for about the last half year. I’ve back tested it till 2020 and its results are the photo. I wanted to use it with prop firms to make some cash since the potential profit with them is way higher than using my own cash, but the drawdowns are too severe. And prop firms are hella strict on their drawdowns.
I’ve run the math and if I scale down enough to where I can trade without blowing up my account, at the rate of withdrawals and taxes and splits, I’d be working for nothing. Min wage basically. And if I scale up then I just blow up accounts due to drawdown. I don’t care about profit either. I need smoothness. Even if I take a major hit in points, so long as it becomes smoother that’s still fine since I can just scale up on prop firms.

It’s basic metrics right now are
WR: 53.78%
Profit Factor: 1.36
Trades Per Day: 8.33

There’s a lot more data but that’s the gist.
The strategy is a blend so it’s RR varies, but it’s pretty close to 1:1RR

—————————-
Here’s my main question: the strategy currently averages 8 trades a day. That’s a lot. Surely I can cut that down to 1-2 and increase the winrate right, therefore increasing its smoothness right? Surely. I’ve tried a lot of things but I’ve failed all analytical approaches. I’ve done a lot of digging and I think I’m just out of my element here.

I think my request is gonna fall on deaf ears but I’m hopeful. Maybe someone earnest and smarter than me can help.

Early replies (first 3)

62220033
BUmp before I sleep

62220056
buy an ad grok

62222767
>>62220056
Does my post sound like an Ai made it lol? It’s a Genuine question

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62222823
>>62219625
my most successful strategies don't care about high winrate, they just win big on a few and cut losses early on the rest. so in that case, less trades = less chances to win big.

62222917
>>62219625
>Surely I can cut that down to 1-2 and increase the winrate right, therefore increasing its smoothness right?
you'll actually get a smoother equity curve if you take more trades but lower risk-per-trade. if you trade less you'll have more luck-based variance in your equity curve.

on win-rate & RR: 50% win rate and 1:1RR really isnt good. its much easier to increase to 2:1RR than it is to try to control your win-rate over large amounts of trades.

i think your best path would be to choose the top 1-3 strategies you have and either increase your RR or try to get your win-rate towards 70% in a scalping style.

62223260
ive typed up like 5 responses but 4chan keeps saying its spam so im shortening my responses a LOT to see if it'll be let thru.

62222823
How do you apply that mechanically? When do you cut losses early? In a large sample, some trades go deep red and still win. Every time I’ve tested early cuts, winrate drops.

62222917
I wanted to filter bad trades using some pre-entry quality metric, but it doesn’t seem that simple.

I’m also not sure how taking more trades with lower risk smooths equity, since scaling up with prop firms brings the drawdown risk back anyway.

I agree the winrate and RR aren’t great, but it’s the best I’ve found. Most people claiming 2:1 RR don’t really hold full size to 2R. They trim early, or if they do hold, their winrate is often too low for prop firm rules.

It’s hard to increase RR without hurting winrate. Every TP or SL adjustment I’ve tested makes winrate suffer. All the lines perform similarly. They help fill drawdown gaps, but also create the risk of all losing on the same day.

I don’t need huge returns, just a smooth strategy with high enough winrate and decent RR to consistently reach payouts.

62223406
>>62223260
>I’m also not sure how taking more trades with lower risk smooths equity
i explained this in my post, you encounter more luck-based variance on the upside and downside with lower trade volume. if your 95% quartile losing streak is 10-15 trades in a row you'll be in a smaller drawdown if your average risk per trade is smaller.

as a general rule, your win-rate and RR exist on a sliding scale. on one end you win 20% but its 5:1 RR and the other end you win 90% but its 0.25:1 RR. basically pick your poison and make sure your trading is actually consistent (not taking trades outside your system).

im a professional futures trader (CTA) and my system averages 35% win rate but 3:1 RR. i've used the same style to pass prop firm evaluations in the past but like you've eluded to, its not the best deal and you are limited by their specific rules. sometimes i have 60%+ win-rate over 1-2 weeks and other weeks i dont get a single win. overall i make money for my clients and return between 80%-120% annualized.

62223681
>>62223406
I’d definitely prefer to lean toward the higher winrate system. With prop firms, blowing accounts is kind of expected. Even if a strategy backtests badly long term, as long as it has strong win streaks before the inevitable losses, I feel like I can engineer it to pass evals, reach a payout, and make money before the losses hit.

So I don’t necessarily need something that wins long term. I need something that wins consistently enough to get to a payout. That difference feels important enough that there should be something workable there, no?

Even with my current strategy, it’s theoretically profitable over time, but like the screenshot shows, it can still go through drawdowns of a few hundred to a few thousand points. Unless I have a huge cushion on the prop firm account, there’s a good chance I blow the account before the edge plays out.

That’s why I prefer a high winrate system. If I can reach a payout first, I don’t really care if the account eventually blows after that. If I’m spending $100–$200 on an account plus resets, but can pull out $1k–$2k, then that’s worth it to me.

How come you have clients? Why not just trade for yourself and keep on scaling up?

62223765
>>62223681
sounds like you want to be a scalper, nothing wrong with that but its limiting for maximizing profits. whats difficult with scalping is being glued to the screen & being paranoid of missing trades by seconds.

If you're only trading NQ or ES mostly on the long side, of course backtesting & optimizing will tell you to just hold through drawdown because it always returns to all time highs. this is a phenomenon that only occurs in US stocks and its teaching you bad habits for trading. a good trader always minimizes losses when they're wrong and seeks to maximize profits when they're right. especially for a 1:1 RR system, its devastating to realize losing trades that are 2+ risk units from your average because it takes so many wins to gain that 1 big loss back.

>How come you have clients? Why not just trade for yourself and keep on scaling up?
even though i have clients, i basically have complete freedom and discretion for my trading within a certain defined range of the 8 markets i trade and risk levels. I write the rules I want. Im doing this as a career & everyone in the finance industry knows that you get rich with other people's money. most of my networth is invested along side my clients so im just gaining greater scale this way. I can bring on client accounts with millions of dollars way faster than what I can earn risking my own capital, the scaling is on another level.

62223860
>>62223765
Depends on what you mean by scalper. The strategies I’m using usually hold positions anywhere from 30 minutes to a few hours, sometimes the whole market day. They’re not super short-term in-and-out trades.

Since they’re rule-based and mechanical, the ideal setup would be to automate entries and step away. I’m not a fan of discretionary trading. I don’t need the whole pie, or even a slice. I just want a few crumbs and make some money and retire early.

>backtesting & optimizing will tell you to just hold through drawdown because it always returns to all-time highs
That is a valid concern but it doesn't apply to my strategy to my knowledge. One way I tested my strategy was through random 30-day simulations and pure 30-day sims.
Example:
Pick a random date walk forward 30 days while trading each day.
Or pick a random day and trade it pick another random day and trade it repeat for 30 days.

In both cases, the strategy was profitable overall. Around 75% of the sims ended green. The picture shows those sims.

If I had deep pockets, this would be simple: just run the strategy live and let it work. If I had traded just 1 NQ contract per trade since its inception around 5 years ago, I’d be looking at nearly $1M in profit, with the worst drawdowns around $40k–$50k. The math goes that I'd need about 50-100K to start, which is no small amount.

The issue is I don’t have that kind of capital. If I trade it properly with my current funds, I’d basically be making pennies. That’s why I need to make it work through prop firms at least to start out.

Again, I ain't asking anyone for money. I am asking if anyone here has knowledge with statistics and math, or some quantitative background. the strat takes 8 damn trades a day, surely there's some way to cut the fluff down to just a few high quality ones.

62224145
bump. y'all make posts about wanting an easy way out but no one puts in the work. ive been trying to make something work for years at this point and i feel like im on the cusp of something, but i realize what im looking for is outside my knowledge. i aint smart enough for this. ive tried so many methods but im hitting dead ends. i need the help of someone who's smarter than me here

62224160
>>62224145
I'm working on the same thing but I have no interest in helping anyone, why would I do that?

62224188
>>62224160
Fair point. The way I see it, I don’t mind if you know the strategy, especially if you end up helping me. That’s the least I could do.

My goal isn’t to milk it forever. I just want to use it to reach a point where I can retire and live a simple, happy life. After that, whatever happens to the strategies or lines doesn’t really matter to me. If you wanted to keep using them and scale them further, I’d be fine with that.

You mentioned you were working on something similar. How far did you get with it? What kind of metrics or performance were you seeing?

62224200
>>62219625
post pseudo code

62224207
>>62224200
the strategies are a mixture of VWAP and ORB. i used a base idea and iterated from there, testing different rule sets and requirements before deciding on the current setup. do you have a background in statistics or quantitative analysis?

62224225
>>62223860
holding for seconds or minutes at a time is usually a scalper. if you're holding for 30 mins to hours+ i dont think 1:1RR is efficient, there has to be some way to cut your risk spacing and achieve the same reward.

clearly you have some type of edge. you could either trade your own capital in MNQ contracts or try to make the drawdowns fit for the prop firm plan. you dont have to trade full size NQs you could have a lot more flexibility with MNQ.

>strat takes 8 damn trades a day, surely there's some way to cut the fluff down to just a few high quality ones
this is where you have to drill down to every trade taken and qualitatively sort out the lower quality trades. theres certainly nuances and details that differ across the trades taken so finding a better way to filter out the bad ones should yield better efficiency. you cant do that type of work through top down back-testing it has to be done trade by trade.

62224267
>>62224188
I have it working, it was running live for a few weeks. Made me an average 1% per day, but upon the first sign of danger I panicked and turned it off. Working on retooling it to notify me and I can configure it on the fly.

62224277
>>62224225

>there has to be some way to cut your risk spacing

Well anon, that’s basically the core problem. I don’t know how to qualitatively filter out lower-quality trades.

I’ve tried pulling data at the exact point of entry, things like EntryFromOpenPct, EntryFromVWAPPct, Prior10RangePct, Prior30ChurnPct, DayRangeSoFarPct, ES confirmation, EMA state, signal candle body/range, stop distance, target distance, planned RR, and a few other metrics.

The idea was to see if winners or losers had anything consistent in common, then build some kind of grading system to decide before entry whether a trade was worth taking. But that ended up being a dead end too. Although I am still testing variants of that as we speak with harsher filters, I'm not super hopeful.

I also don’t want to rely entirely on ChatGPT or AI to generate ideas, because it could easily lead me astray and I wouldn’t know, since I’m out of my element here. That’s why I’m stuck.

>you don’t have to trade full-size NQs, you could have more flexibility with MNQ

I looked into that too. The issue is that if I size down enough to survive the drawdowns, my scale becomes too small. It would take weeks or months to pass evaluations, then even more time to reach payouts.

And once I run the math, I’d be spending 3–5 hours a day trading for basically nothing. Not even $100/day in actual profit after costs and resets.

So technically, I do have a path to payouts right now. The problem is the math says it’s just not worth it.

>top down back-testing it has to be done trade by trade.

Sometimes if I have an idea, I’ll manually backtest it through a few days or weeks first. If it shows promise, then I’ll programmatically test it across the full dataset.

There are definitely some strategies that work well in specific regimes. The problem is that by the time you realize the regime has shifted, you’ve usually already given back most or all of what you made from it.

62224392
>>62224277
that sounds like the right path to be taking to find that extra quality filter. maybe you need more testing time to find that last filter piece. i've learned that more criteria is not always yielding more efficient results though, you can end up filtering out alot of good trades and reducing trade volume too much. i dont think AI is good for idea generation for trading or signals, personally i use AI to run all the math stats and simulations exclusively.

>would take weeks or months to pass evaluations, then even more time to reach payouts.
I passed most evaluations in 1 week to 8 weeks. i feel like if you're trying to pass in less than a week your size is prob too big. the 1 week pass was during a highly volatile period where i got much bigger wins.

It seems like you're on the right track. in trading, usually just doing 1 or 2 things really really well is better than having too many tools. i still disagree with your core stats of 1:1RR and requirements of high win-rate. i know theres traders that make that work but i've always wanted 1 big win to pay for 3+ losers. the big fish arent always biting but when you land 1 you realize chasing small fish all the time is a waste.

62224555
>>62224392
Everything you mentioned: sizing down, different contract sizes, different RR models, higher RR/lower win rate systems, and higher win rate/lower RR systems… I’ve already considered, tested, or looked into.

The main issue is the math. If it takes me around five weeks to pass a typical $50,000 evaluation with a $3,000 profit target, that works out to about $120 per trading day. If I then use the same strategy on a funded account, which makes sense since the rules are usually very similar, that same strategy is still only producing about $120 per day. After the prop firm split and taxes, that is well under $100 per day, which is not really viable long term or enough for financial independence.

The only way to make that work would be running multiple accounts, but that creates the same problem, just spread across many accounts all of which cost money to start and activate. If the strategy hits a deeper drawdown period, I could lose all of them at once.

So the issue is that I either size down so much that I spend thousands on evals, resets, and activation fees just to withdraw a few hundred dollars later, or I need to find a strategy that can grow more aggressively.

That is why I’m focused on a higher win rate + a higher profit factor. A lower win rate strategy might still have good EV on paper, but it also means more losing streaks and more resets along the way. A higher win rate gives me a better chance to build a cushion before those rough periods come.

With that being said, I’m not going to pretend I know everything. After all, I’m the one who came to this board asking for help.

The truth is, I don’t currently have a strategy with the kind of stats you’re describing (3:1RR) The one I have now, with its current win rate and profit factor, is the best I’ve been able to build so far hence why I’ve been sticking with it.

62224557
>>62224267
1% a day means 500% returns after just half a year. If you got a strategy like that then you’d be flying in a private jet within a few years lmao

item.title: Do I give up WFH for 50 percent raise and a job that will be good for my career or do I stay WFH for poverty wage(100k)… — 17 replies — 12.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do I give up WFH for 50 percent raise and a job that will be good for my career or do I stay WFH for poverty wage(100k)…

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-13 06:08 UTC

OP

Do I give up WFH for 50 percent raise and a job that will be good for my career or do I stay WFH for poverty wage(100k) and focus on finishing my masters and not huffing fumes in traffic?


I feel like i wont regret giving up wfh later in life but I'll be miserable for the next 3 years.. I feel like im fucked either way.

Early replies (first 3)

62222340
>>62222336
Meant to say later in life like 3 years later when I quit cuz I cant go in the office my while life, id rather be dead. But it might be worth it to suck it up for a few years

62222349
What's the masters in? This is the hinge the decision should be on.

62222450
>>62222336
Consider how much money you would spend on gas commuting everyday for a year, and consider that gas could still go up another 50% from today's prices. So you know that you are spending $3.5k — 5.2k in gas this year citing today's average American gas price for calculations. Is office life worth this sacrifice?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62222880
>>62222336
take the 50% raise wtf

62222939
how long is the gommute

62222960
please kill yourself

62223191
>>62222349
Electrical Engineering

62223303
>>62222939
The goymute is 42 miles so I would have to move or get a tesla with fsd

62223366
Things are about to get COVID style unstable,band unemployment is at 30%. I'd go with whichever job is less likely to terminate early. Any steady paycheck is better than no paycheck.

62223378
>>62223303
I personally would not want to spend 2 hrs a day commuting. That time could be spent doing sports, dating, another side hustle that I really like, etc.

62223393
>>62223303
I would not unless you are open to moving closer.
>>62223366
Also this

I would probably kms if I had to do an actual rigorous masters while working full time in office desu

62223417
>>62222880
Ikr, yet a lot of people here and a big part of myself really want work life balance but 50 percent raise should be a no brainer

62223423
>>62223378
I cant afford to date on 105k a year also im an engineer so what dates?

62223427
>>62223393
I am thinking of killing myself. I am open to moving but its in a liberal hellhole. Gotta do what I gotta do.

62224325
>>62222336
Get a job that's no easily replaced by AI
If you commute buy a Tesla with fsd so you don't have to actively drive and spend the time doing something productive.

62224369
>>62222336
putting up with non whites in person is not worth anything

62224584
>>62223366
In two weeks right?
